summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering two zoning cases, department updates, and community announcements. Zoning case ZC-2025-007 (PLK Communities' request to rezone 28.77 acres from M1/B2 to PDM for a 230-unit multifamily development on Smith Road) was denied 2-1 based on staff and Zoning Commission recommendations citing inconsistency with the Growth Management Plan and State Route 28 Corridor Plan. Zoning case ZC-2025-008 (Victor Capitula's request to rezone ~16 acres from R3 to PDR for 34 ranch-style condominiums on Greenstone Trace) was discussed with staff recommending approval with stipulations, though some residents raised concerns about floodplain building, stormwater runoff, and lack of direct notification.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously (3-0)
Motion to approve payment of bills (October 28 - November 18, 2025) passed unanimously (3-0)
Motion to deny zoning case ZC-2025-007 (PLK Communities rezoning from M1/B2 to PDM for 230-unit multifamily development) passed 2-1 (Allen yes, Hauserman yes, Hodges no)
action_items: Staff to continue processing zoning case ZC-2025-008 (Greenstone Trace condominiums) with stipulations including minimum 5,000 sq ft lot configuration, sidewalks on both sides of streets, and HOA maintenance documentation
Fire department working on replacement/repair of Medic 2-18 (chassis likely totaled, 6-12 months for return)
Service department completing Station 18 construction - target move-in March, grand opening April after final paving
Address procedural management items from completed 2023-2024 audit
key_amounts: 230 residential units proposed in PLK Communities development (denied)
489 parking spaces proposed in PLK Communities development
34 residential units (17 paired ranch-style condominiums) proposed for Greenstone Trace
52% open space in Greenstone Trace development
65% open space in PLK Communities development
7.99 dwelling units per acre proposed by PLK Communities (under 8 max)
notable_topics: Denial of PLK Communities 230-unit multifamily rezoning on Smith Road (ZC-2025-007)
Review of Greenstone Trace 34-unit condominium development (ZC-2025-008) with resident opposition regarding floodplain concerns and stormwater runoff
State Route 28 Corridor Plan and Growth Management Plan applicability debate
Station 18 construction progress - steps going in, exhaust system started, floors being polished, March move-in target
Medic 2-18 chassis likely totaled after being towed to Horton in Columbus
Strategic planning work session held with township leadership teams
Holly Fair weekend events and Light Up Goshen parade
Return of Army Corporal Kibbe's remains from Korea
Prayers for Casey McLean after serious accident
attendees: Nick Wingenfelter (PLK Communities, Chief Development Officer)
Chip Brown (Plumtree Realtor, sales manager for Greenstone Trace project)
notes: The PLK Communities zoning case had a split vote with Trustee Hodges dissenting on the denial. The applicant argued the property was primarily B2-zoned (not M1) and that their plan aligned with the State Route 28 Corridor Plan, citing the Drees development across the street as legal precedent. Staff position appeared to shift from earlier favorable analysis to denial recommendation. The transcript was truncated before the conclusion of zoning case ZC-2025-008 discussion and vote. Residents of Greenstone Trace raised significant concerns about floodplain construction, stormwater management, lack of direct notification (notices went to HOA rather than individual unit owners), and developer accountability.
title: BOT 11-18-25
